690	/-! ### Adjunction transposition of the descent-equalizer legs
691	
692	The overlap condition consumed by `glueLift` is an equation between composites of adjoint
693	transposes along the chart immersions. The next two lemmas transpose each leg back across
694	the pullback–pushforward adjunction of the *composite* overlap immersion, exposing the
695	pullback-level identity `g_{ij} ∘ f_ij^* u^i = (t_ij ≫ f_ji)^* u^j` that the matrix
696	computation closes. The first handles the unit/`pushforwardComp` factor pair, the second
697	the trailing `pushforwardCongr` reindexing cast. -/
698	
699	/-- **Leg transpose** (`lem:gr_tautologicalQuotientComponent_transpose` engine): for
700	`b : V ⟶ U`, `a : U ⟶ X` and `c : a^* W ⟶ M`, the descent-equalizer leg
701	`homEquiv_a(c) ≫ (a_* unit_b) ≫ pushforwardComp` is the transpose along the composite
702	`b ≫ a` of the pullback of `c` to `V` (through the pseudofunctor comparison
703	`pullbackComp`). Combines `homEquiv_conjugateEquiv_app` with Mathlib's
704	`conjugateEquiv_pullbackComp_inv`. Project-local. -/
705	lemma homEquiv_comp_unit_pushforwardComp {V U X : Scheme.{u}} (b : V ⟶ U) (a : U ⟶ X)
706	    {W : X.Modules} {M : U.Modules} (c : (Scheme.Modules.pullback a).obj W ⟶ M) :
707	    (Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction a).homEquiv W M c ≫
708	        ((Scheme.Modules.pushforward a).map
709	          ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction b).unit.app M) ≫
710	        (Scheme.Modules.pushforwardComp b a).hom.app ((Scheme.Modules.pullback b).obj M))
711	      = (Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction (b ≫ a)).homEquiv W
712	          ((Scheme.Modules.pullback b).obj M)
713	          ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp b a).inv.app W ≫ (Scheme.Modules.pullback b).map c) := by
714	  -- inner transpose: `c ≫ unit_b` is the `b`-transpose of `(pullback b).map c`
715	  have h2 : (Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction b).homEquiv
716	        ((Scheme.Modules.pullback a).obj W) ((Scheme.Modules.pullback b).obj M)
717	        ((Scheme.Modules.pullback b).map c)
718	      = c ≫ (Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction b).unit.app M := by
719	    rw [Adjunction.homEquiv_unit]
720	    exact ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction b).unit.naturality c).symm
721	  -- composite-adjunction transpose factors through the two single transposes
722	  have h3 : ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction a).comp
723	        (Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction b)).homEquiv W
724	        ((Scheme.Modules.pullback b).obj M) ((Scheme.Modules.pullback b).map c)
725	      = (Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction a).homEquiv _ _
726	          ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction b).homEquiv _ _
727	            ((Scheme.Modules.pullback b).map c)) := by
728	    rw [Adjunction.comp_homEquiv]
729	    rfl
730	  -- mate compatibility: precomposing with `pullbackComp.inv` is postcomposition by the
731	  -- conjugate, which Mathlib identifies as `pushforwardComp.hom`
732	  have h4 := homEquiv_conjugateEquiv_app
733	      ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction a).comp
734	        (Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction b))
735	      (Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction (b ≫ a))
736	      (Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp b a).inv
737	      (f := (Scheme.Modules.pullback b).map c)
738	  rw [Scheme.Modules.conjugateEquiv_pullbackComp_inv] at h4
739	  rw [h4, h3, h2]
740	  -- regroup and fold the unit factor back into the transpose (term-mode: a positional
741	  -- `rw [homEquiv_naturality_right]` matches inside the wrong `homEquiv` argument)
742	  exact (Category.assoc _ _ _).symm.trans
743	    (congrArg (· ≫ (Scheme.Modules.pushforwardComp b a).hom.app
744	        ((Scheme.Modules.pullback b).obj M))
745	      (Adjunction.homEquiv_naturality_right _ _ _).symm)
746	
747	/-- **Congruence-cast transpose**: postcomposing a transpose along `e` with the
748	`pushforwardCongr` cast of an equality `e = e'` is the transpose along `e'` of the
749	`pullbackCongr`-reindexed morphism. Generic `subst` lemma. Project-local. -/
750	lemma homEquiv_comp_pushforwardCongr {V X : Scheme.{u}} {e e' : V ⟶ X} (h : e = e')
751	    {W : X.Modules} {N : V.Modules} (y : (Scheme.Modules.pullback e).obj W ⟶ N) :
752	    (Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction e).homEquiv W N y ≫
753	        (Scheme.Modules.pushforwardCongr h).hom.app N
754	      = (Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction e').homEquiv W N
755	          ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackCongr h).inv.app W ≫ y) := by
756	  subst h
757	  have h1 : (Scheme.Modules.pushforwardCongr (rfl : e = e)).hom.app N = 𝟙 _ := by
758	    ext U
759	    simp
760	  have h2 : (Scheme.Modules.pullbackCongr (rfl : e = e)).inv.app W = 𝟙 _ := by
761	    simp [Scheme.Modules.pullbackCongr]
762	  rw [h1, h2, Category.comp_id, Category.id_comp]
763	
764	/-- **Transposed form of the `glueLift` overlap condition**: the `(i,j)`-component of the
765	equalizing hypothesis consumed by `glueLift` holds iff the pullback-level identity
766	`f_ij^* (c i) = congr ∘ (t_ij ≫ f_ji)^* (c j) ∘ g_ij⁻¹` does (all comparisons through the
767	pseudofunctor casts). Both legs are transposed along the composite overlap immersion via
768	`homEquiv_comp_unit_pushforwardComp` / `homEquiv_comp_pushforwardCongr`, and the
769	hom-equivalence is injective. Project-local. -/
770	lemma glueLift_cond_iff (D : Scheme.GlueData.{u})
771	    (M : ∀ i, (D.U i).Modules)
772	    (g : ∀ i j, (Scheme.Modules.pullback (D.f i j)).obj (M i) ≅
773	        (Scheme.Modules.pullback (D.t i j ≫ D.f j i)).obj (M j))
774	    {W : D.glued.Modules}
775	    (c : ∀ i, (Scheme.Modules.pullback (D.ι i)).obj W ⟶ M i) (i j : D.J) :
776	    ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction (D.ι i)).homEquiv W (M i) (c i) ≫
777	        ((Scheme.Modules.pushforward (D.ι i)).map
778	          ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction (D.f i j)).unit.app (M i)) ≫
779	        (Scheme.Modules.pushforwardComp (D.f i j) (D.ι i)).hom.app
780	          ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (D.f i j)).obj (M i)))
781	      = (Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction (D.ι j)).homEquiv W (M j) (c j) ≫
782	        ((Scheme.Modules.pushforward (D.ι j)).map
783	          ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction

785	        (Scheme.Modules.pushforwardComp (D.t i j ≫ D.f j i) (D.ι j)).hom.app
786	          ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (D.t i j ≫ D.f j i)).obj (M j)) ≫
787	        (Scheme.Modules.pushforward
788	          ((D.t i j ≫ D.f j i) ≫ D.ι j)).map (g i j).inv ≫
789	        (Scheme.Modules.pushforwardCongr
790	          (show (D.t i j ≫ D.f j i) ≫ D.ι j = D.f i j ≫ D.ι i by
791	            rw [Category.assoc]; exact D.glue_condition i j)).hom.app
792	          ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (D.f i j)).obj (M i))))
793	    ↔ ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp (D.f i j) (D.ι i)).inv.app W ≫
794	          (Scheme.Modules.pullback (D.f i j)).map (c i)
795	      = (Scheme.Modules.pullbackCongr
796	            (show (D.t i j ≫ D.f j i) ≫ D.ι j = D.f i j ≫ D.ι i by
797	              rw [Category.assoc]; exact D.glue_condition i j)).inv.app W ≫
798	          (Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp (D.t i j ≫ D.f j i) (D.ι j)).inv.app W ≫
799	          (Scheme.Modules.pullback (D.t i j ≫ D.f j i)).map (c j) ≫ (g i j).inv) := by
800	  -- transpose the left leg

802	  -- transpose the right leg: regroup, fire the leg transpose, absorb `(g i j).inv`
803	  -- into the transpose, then fire the congruence-cast transpose

805	        ((Scheme.Modules.pushforward (D.ι j)).map
806	          ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction
807	            (D.t i j ≫ D.f j i)).unit.app (M j)) ≫
808	        (Scheme.Modules.pushforwardComp (D.t i j ≫ D.f j i) (D.ι j)).hom.app
809	          ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (D.t i j ≫ D.f j i)).obj (M j)) ≫
810	        (Scheme.Modules.pushforward
811	          ((D.t i j ≫ D.f j i) ≫ D.ι j)).map (g i j).inv ≫
812	        (Scheme.Modules.pushforwardCongr
813	          (show (D.t i j ≫ D.f j i) ≫ D.ι j = D.f i j ≫ D.ι i by
814	            rw [Category.assoc]; exact D.glue_condition i j)).hom.app
815	          ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (D.f i j)).obj (M i)))
816	      = (Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction (D.f i j ≫ D.ι i)).homEquiv W
817	          ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (D.f i j)).obj (M i))
818	          ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackCongr
819	              (show (D.t i j ≫ D.f j i) ≫ D.ι j = D.f i j ≫ D.ι i by
820	                rw [Category.assoc]; exact D.glue_condition i j)).inv.app W ≫
821	            (((Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp (D.t i j ≫ D.f j i) (D.ι j)).inv.app W ≫

891	/-! ### Restriction of the glued sheaf to a chart (`def:gr_modules_glueRestrictionIso`)
892	
893	The glued sheaf is the descent equalizer `eq(a, b) ⊆ ∏ᵢ (ιᵢ)_* Mᵢ`. This section
894	re-exposes the two legs of that equalizer as standalone declarations
895	(`glueLegA`/`glueLegB`), records that `glue` *is* their equalizer (`glueIsoEqualizer`,
896	definitional), and produces the canonical projection `glueProj i` of the glued sheaf
897	onto the `i`-th pushforward factor together with its compatibility with `glueLift`.
898	The adjoint transpose of `glueProj i` along the chart immersion `D.ι i` is the
899	*restriction morphism* `glueRestrictionHom i : ιᵢ^* (glue …) ⟶ M i`; effective descent
900	(consuming the cocycle hypotheses C1/C2) makes it an isomorphism — the restriction
901	isomorphism `glueRestrictionIso` of `def:gr_modules_glueRestrictionIso`. The
902	limit-preservation engine is `restrictFunctor`: pullback along an open immersion is
903	naturally isomorphic to a site-level pushforward, which is a right adjoint, hence
904	preserves the descent equalizer and the pushforward product. -/
905	
906	/-- `restrictFunctor f` along an open immersion is a right adjoint: it is a site-level
907	pushforward of sheaves of modules, whose left adjoint (the site-level pullback) exists
908	by the presheaf-pullback + sheafification construction. Project-local instance. -/
909	instance restrictFunctor_isRightAdjoint {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (f : X ⟶ Y) [IsOpenImmersion f] :

911	  delta restrictFunctor
912	  -- bare `infer_instance` fails on the outer search; the explicit presheaf-pullback +
913	  -- sheafification construction elaborates (its three instance hypotheses all resolve)
914	  exact (SheafOfModules.PullbackConstruction.adjunction _).isRightAdjoint

934	-- NOTE: `glue`/`glueLift` elaborated universe-monomorphic at `Scheme.GlueData.{0}`
935	-- (their universe was pinned during elaboration); the restriction layer follows suit.
936	variable (D : Scheme.GlueData.{0}) (M : ∀ i, (D.U i).Modules)
937	
938	/-- The product of pushforwards `∏ᵢ (ιᵢ)_* Mᵢ` into which the glued sheaf embeds.
939	Project-local helper re-exposing the `P` of `glue`. -/
940	noncomputable def glueProd : D.glued.Modules :=
941	  ∏ᶜ fun i => (Scheme.Modules.pushforward (D.ι i)).obj (M i)
942	
943	/-- The overlap product `∏_{(i,j)} (f_ij ≫ ιᵢ)_* (f_ij^* Mᵢ)` receiving the two descent
944	legs. Project-local helper re-exposing the `Qfun`-product of `glue`. -/
945	noncomputable def glueOverlapProd : D.glued.Modules :=
946	  ∏ᶜ fun p : D.J × D.J =>
947	    (Scheme.Modules.pushforward (D.f p.1 p.2 ≫ D.ι p.1)).obj
948	      ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (D.f p.1 p.2)).obj (M p.1))
949	
950	/-- First descent leg (`a` of `glue`): on the `(i,j)`-component, restrict the `i`-th
951	chart section to the overlap `V (i,j)` via the unit of the pullback–pushforward
952	adjunction along `f_ij` and the pushforward-composition comparison. Project-local. -/
953	noncomputable def glueLegA : glueProd D M ⟶ glueOverlapProd D M :=
954	  Pi.lift fun p => Pi.π _ p.1 ≫
955	    ((Scheme.Modules.pushforward (D.ι p.1)).map
956	        ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction (D.f p.1 p.2)).unit.app (M p.1)) ≫
957	      (Scheme.Modules.pushforwardComp (D.f p.1 p.2) (D.ι p.1)).hom.app
958	        ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (D.f p.1 p.2)).obj (M p.1)))
959	
960	/-- Second descent leg (`b` of `glue`): on the `(i,j)`-component, restrict the `j`-th
961	chart section, transport it across the transition isomorphism `g_ij`, and reindex the
962	immersion via the glue condition. Project-local. -/
963	noncomputable def glueLegB
964	    (g : ∀ i j, (Scheme.Modules.pullback (D.f i j)).obj (M i) ≅
965	        (Scheme.Modules.pullback (D.t i j ≫ D.f j i)).obj (M j)) :
966	    glueProd D M ⟶ glueOverlapProd D M :=
967	  Pi.lift fun p => Pi.π _ p.2 ≫
968	    ((Scheme.Modules.pushforward (D.ι p.2)).map
969	        ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction
970	          (D.t p.1 p.2 ≫ D.f p.2 p.1)).unit.app (M p.2)) ≫
971	      (Scheme.Modules.pushforwardComp (D.t p.1 p.2 ≫ D.f p.2 p.1) (D.ι p.2)).hom.app
972	        ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (D.t p.1 p.2 ≫ D.f p.2 p.1)).obj (M p.2)) ≫
973	      (Scheme.Modules.pushforward
974	        ((D.t p.1 p.2 ≫ D.f p.2 p.1) ≫ D.ι p.2)).map (g p.1 p.2).inv ≫
975	      (Scheme.Modules.pushforwardCongr
976	        (show (D.t p.1 p.2 ≫ D.f p.2 p.1) ≫ D.ι p.2 = D.f p.1 p.2 ≫ D.ι p.1 by
977	          rw [Category.assoc]; exact D.glue_condition p.1 p.2)).hom.app
978	        ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (D.f p.1 p.2)).obj (M p.1)))
979	
980	variable (g : ∀ i j, (Scheme.Modules.pullback (D.f i j)).obj (M i) ≅
981	      (Scheme.Modules.pullback (D.t i j ≫ D.f j i)).obj (M j))
982	  (hC1 : ∀ i, g i i = eqToIso (congrArg (fun φ => (Scheme.Modules.pullback φ).obj (M i))
983	      (show D.f i i = D.t i i ≫ D.f i i by rw [D.t_id i, Category.id_comp])))
984	  (hC2 : ∀ i j k,
985	      pullbackBaseChangeTransport (pullback.fst (D.f i j) (D.f i k))
986	          (D.f i j) (D.t i j ≫ D.f j i) (g i j) ≪≫
987	        (Scheme.Modules.pullbackCongr (glueData_bridge_mid D i j k)).app (M j) ≪≫
988	        pullbackBaseChangeTransport (D.t' i j k ≫ pullback.fst (D.f j k) (D.f j i))
989	          (D.f j k) (D.t j k ≫ D.f k j) (g j k) ≪≫
990	        (Scheme.Modules.pullbackCongr (glueData_bridge_tgt D i j k)).app (M k)
991	      = (Scheme.Modules.pullbackCongr (glueData_bridge_src D i j k)).app (M i) ≪≫
992	        pullbackBaseChangeTransport (pullback.snd (D.f i j) (D.f i k))
993	          (D.f i k) (D.t i k ≫ D.f k i) (g i k))

995	/-- The glued sheaf *is* the equalizer of the two (re-exposed) descent legs. The
996	isomorphism is definitional (`Iso.refl`); it exists to let the equalizer API fire
997	syntactically on `glue`. Project-local. -/
998	noncomputable def glueIsoEqualizer :
999	    glue D M g hC1 hC2 ≅ equalizer (glueLegA D M) (glueLegB D M g) :=
1000	  Iso.refl _

1002	/-- Projection of the glued sheaf onto the `i`-th pushforward factor `(ιᵢ)_* Mᵢ`:
1003	the equalizer inclusion followed by the product projection. Project-local. -/

1005	    glue D M g hC1 hC2 ⟶ (Scheme.Modules.pushforward (D.ι i)).obj (M i) :=
1006	  (glueIsoEqualizer D M g hC1 hC2).hom ≫ equalizer.ι (glueLegA D M) (glueLegB D M g) ≫

1014	    (hk : ∀ p : D.J × D.J,
1015	      k p.1 ≫
1016	          ((Scheme.Modules.pushforward (D.ι p.1)).map
1017	            ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction (D.f p.1 p.2)).unit.app (M p.1)) ≫
1018	          (Scheme.Modules.pushforwardComp (D.f p.1 p.2) (D.ι p.1)).hom.app
1019	            ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (D.f p.1 p.2)).obj (M p.1)))
1020	        = k p.2 ≫
1021	          ((Scheme.Modules.pushforward (D.ι p.2)).map
1022	            ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction
1023	              (D.t p.1 p.2 ≫ D.f p.2 p.1)).unit.app (M p.2)) ≫
1024	          (Scheme.Modules.pushforwardComp (D.t p.1 p.2 ≫ D.f p.2 p.1) (D.ι p.2)).hom.app
1025	            ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (D.t p.1 p.2 ≫ D.f p.2 p.1)).obj (M p.2)) ≫

1028	          (Scheme.Modules.pushforwardCongr
1029	            (show (D.t p.1 p.2 ≫ D.f p.2 p.1) ≫ D.ι p.2 = D.f p.1 p.2 ≫ D.ι p.1 by
1030	              rw [Category.assoc]; exact D.glue_condition p.1 p.2)).hom.app
1031	            ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (D.f p.1 p.2)).obj (M p.1)))) (i : D.J) :
1032	    glueLift D M g hC1 hC2 k hk ≫ glueProj D M g hC1 hC2 i = k i := by
1033	  dsimp only [glueLift, glueProj, glueIsoEqualizer, Iso.refl_hom]
1034	  -- term-mode: the mixed-provenance comp nodes block positional `rw [Category.id_comp]`

1036	    ((Category.assoc _ _ _).symm.trans
1037	      ((congrArg (· ≫ Pi.π _ i) (equalizer.lift_ι _ _)).trans (Limits.Pi.lift_π _ _)))

1040	transpose, along the chart immersion `ιᵢ`, of the `i`-th projection `glueProj i`.
1041	Effective descent (`isIso_glueRestrictionHom`) makes it an isomorphism. Project-local. -/

1043	    (Scheme.Modules.pullback (D.ι i)).obj (glue D M g hC1 hC2) ⟶ M i :=
1044	  ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction (D.ι i)).homEquiv _ _).symm

1045	    (glueProj D M g hC1 hC2 i)

1065	    (Scheme.Modules.pullback (D.ι i)).obj (glueProd D M)

1103	/-- **Overlap-square opens identity**: for an open `V` of the `i`-th chart of a scheme
1104	glue datum, the preimage under `ι_j` of its image in the glued scheme coincides with
1105	the image, under the `j`-side overlap immersion `t_ij ≫ f_ji`, of its preimage under
1106	`f_ij`. This is the underlying-opens form of the cartesianness of the chart-overlap
1107	square (`vPullbackCone`); it is the site-level input identifying the two composite
1108	restriction functors of the overlap square. Project-local. -/
1109	lemma glueData_preimage_image_eq (D : Scheme.GlueData.{0}) (i j : D.J)
1110	    (V : (D.U i).Opens) :
1111	    (D.ι j) ⁻¹ᵁ ((D.ι i) ''ᵁ V) = (D.t i j ≫ D.f j i) ''ᵁ ((D.f i j) ⁻¹ᵁ V) := by
1112	  ext x
1113	  constructor
1114	  · intro hx
1115	    -- a point of `U_j` mapping into `ι_i(V)` comes from the overlap via the glue relation
1116	    obtain ⟨y, hyV, hyx⟩ := hx
1117	    obtain ⟨w, hw1, hw2⟩ := (D.ι_eq_iff i j y x).mp hyx
1118	    exact ⟨w, show (D.f i j) w ∈ V from (show (D.f i j) w = y from hw1) ▸ hyV, hw2⟩
1119	  · rintro ⟨w, hwV, rfl⟩
1120	    refine ⟨D.f i j w, hwV, ?_⟩
1121	    -- `ι_i (f_ij w) = ι_j ((t_ij ≫ f_ji) w)`: the glue condition at the point `w`
1122	    have h := congrArg (fun m : D.V (i, j) ⟶ D.glued => m w)
1123	      ((D.glue_condition i j).symm.trans (Category.assoc _ _ _).symm)
1124	    exact h

1206	    IsIso (glueRestrictionHom D M g hC1 hC2 i) := by
1207	  sorry
1208	
1209	/-- **The restriction isomorphism of the glued sheaf**
1210	(`def:gr_modules_glueRestrictionIso`): the canonical identification
1211	`ι_i^* (glue D M g …) ≅ M i` of the chart restriction of the glued sheaf with the
1212	`i`-th input sheaf, with underlying morphism the adjoint transpose of the `i`-th
1213	descent-equalizer projection. Project-local. -/
1214	noncomputable def glueRestrictionIso (D : Scheme.GlueData.{0}) (M : ∀ i, (D.U i).Modules)
1215	    (g : ∀ i j, (Scheme.Modules.pullback (D.f i j)).obj (M i) ≅
1216	        (Scheme.Modules.pullback (D.t i j ≫ D.f j i)).obj (M j))
1217	    (hC1 : ∀ i, g i i = eqToIso (congrArg (fun φ => (Scheme.Modules.pullback φ).obj (M i))
1218	        (show D.f i i = D.t i i ≫ D.f i i by rw [D.t_id i, Category.id_comp])))
1219	    (hC2 : ∀ i j k,
1220	        pullbackBaseChangeTransport (pullback.fst (D.f i j) (D.f i k))
1221	            (D.f i j) (D.t i j ≫ D.f j i) (g i j) ≪≫
1222	          (Scheme.Modules.pullbackCongr (glueData_bridge_mid D i j k)).app (M j) ≪≫
1223	          pullbackBaseChangeTransport (D.t' i j k ≫ pullback.fst (D.f j k) (D.f j i))
1224	            (D.f j k) (D.t j k ≫ D.f k j) (g j k) ≪≫
1225	          (Scheme.Modules.pullbackCongr (glueData_bridge_tgt D i j k)).app (M k)
1226	        = (Scheme.Modules.pullbackCongr (glueData_bridge_src D i j k)).app (M i) ≪≫
1227	          pullbackBaseChangeTransport (pullback.snd (D.f i j) (D.f i k))
1228	            (D.f i k) (D.t i k ≫ D.f k i) (g i k)) (i : D.J) :
1229	    (Scheme.Modules.pullback (D.ι i)).obj (glue D M g hC1 hC2) ≅ M i :=
1230	  haveI := isIso_glueRestrictionHom D M g hC1 hC2 i
1231	  asIso (glueRestrictionHom D M g hC1 hC2 i)
1232	
1233	end AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules
1234

1	/-
2	Copyright (c) 2024 Joël Riou. All rights reserved.
3	Released under Apache 2.0 license as described in the file LICENSE.
4	Authors: Joël Riou
5	-/
6	module
7	
8	public import Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Presheaf.Pushforward
9	public import Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Sheaf
10	public import Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Sites.Over
11	public import Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Comma.Over.Pullback
12	public import Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Limits.Shapes.BinaryProducts
13	
14	/-!
15	# Pushforward of sheaves of modules
16	
17	Assume that categories `C` and `D` are equipped with Grothendieck topologies, and
18	that `F : C ⥤ D` is a continuous functor.
19	Then, if `φ : S ⟶ (F.sheafPushforwardContinuous RingCat.{u} J K).obj R` is
20	a morphism of sheaves of rings, we construct the pushforward functor
21	`pushforward φ : SheafOfModules.{v} R ⥤ SheafOfModules.{v} S`, and
22	we show that they interact with the composition of morphisms similarly as pseudofunctors.
23	
24	-/
25	
26	@[expose] public section
27	
28	universe w v' u' v v₁ v₂ v₃ v₄ u₁ u₂ u₃ u₄ u
29	
30	open CategoryTheory Functor
31	
32	namespace SheafOfModules
33	
34	variable {C : Type u₁} [Category.{v₁} C] {D : Type u₂} [Category.{v₂} D]
35	  {D' : Type u₃} [Category.{v₃} D'] {D'' : Type u₄} [Category.{v₄} D'']
36	  {J : GrothendieckTopology C} {K : GrothendieckTopology D} {F : C ⥤ D}
37	  {S : Sheaf J RingCat.{u}} {R : Sheaf K RingCat.{u}}
38	  [Functor.IsContinuous F J K]
39	  (φ : S ⟶ (F.sheafPushforwardContinuous RingCat.{u} J K).obj R)
40	
41	/-- The pushforward of sheaves of modules that is induced by a continuous functor `F`
42	and a morphism of sheaves of rings `φ : S ⟶ (F.sheafPushforwardContinuous RingCat J K).obj R`. -/
43	@[simps map_val, simps -isSimp obj_val]
44	noncomputable def pushforward : SheafOfModules.{v} R ⥤ SheafOfModules.{v} S where
45	  obj M :=
46	    { val := (PresheafOfModules.pushforward φ.hom).obj M.val
47	      isSheaf := ((F.sheafPushforwardContinuous _ J K).obj ⟨_, M.isSheaf⟩).property }
48	  map f :=
49	    { val := (PresheafOfModules.pushforward φ.hom).map f.val }
50	
51	/-- Given `M : SheafOfModules R` and `X : D`, this is the restriction of `M`
52	over the sheaf of rings `R.over X` on the category `Over X`. -/
53	noncomputable abbrev over (M : SheafOfModules.{v} R) (X : D) : SheafOfModules.{v} (R.over X) :=
54	  (pushforward.{v} (𝟙 _)).obj M
55	
56	/-- Given a map `f : M ⟶ N` between sheaves of modules over `R`, this is the restriction
57	to the map `M.over X ⟶ N.over X` between sheaves of modules over `R.over X`. -/
58	noncomputable abbrev Hom.over {M N : SheafOfModules.{v} R} (f : M ⟶ N) (X : D) :
59	    M.over X ⟶ N.over X :=
60	  (pushforward.{v} (𝟙 _)).map f
61	
62	section
63	
64	variable (R) in
65	/-- The pushforward functor by the identity morphism identifies to
66	the identify functor of the category of sheaves of modules. -/
67	noncomputable def pushforwardId :
68	    pushforward.{v} (S := R) (F := 𝟭 _) (𝟙 R) ≅ 𝟭 _ :=
69	  Iso.refl _
70	
71	/-- Pushforwards along equal morphisms of sheaves of rings are isomorphic. -/
72	noncomputable
73	def pushforwardCongr {φ ψ : S ⟶ (F.sheafPushforwardContinuous RingCat.{u} J K).obj R} (e : φ = ψ) :
74	    pushforward.{v} φ ≅ pushforward.{v} ψ :=
75	  NatIso.ofComponents (fun X ↦ (SheafOfModules.fullyFaithfulForget _).preimageIso
76	    (PresheafOfModules.isoMk (fun U ↦ (ModuleCat.restrictScalarsCongr (by subst e; rfl)).app _)
77	      fun _ _ _ ↦ by subst e; rfl)) fun _ ↦ by subst e; rfl
78	
79	@[simp] lemma pushforwardCongr_symm
80	    {φ ψ : S ⟶ (F.sheafPushforwardContinuous RingCat.{u} J K).obj R} (e : φ = ψ) :
81	  (pushforwardCongr e).symm = pushforwardCongr e.symm := rfl
82	
83	@[simp] lemma pushforwardCongr_hom_app_val_app
84	    {φ ψ : S ⟶ (F.sheafPushforwardContinuous RingCat.{u} J K).obj R} (e : φ = ψ) (M U x) :
85	  ((pushforwardCongr e).hom.app M).val.app U x = x := rfl
86	
87	@[simp] lemma pushforwardCongr_inv_app_val_app
88	    {φ ψ : S ⟶ (F.sheafPushforwardContinuous RingCat.{u} J K).obj R} (e : φ = ψ) (M U x) :
89	  ((pushforwardCongr e).inv.app M).val.app U x = x := rfl
90	
91	section
92	
93	variable {K' : GrothendieckTopology D'} {K'' : GrothendieckTopology D''}
94	  {G : D ⥤ D'} {R' : Sheaf K' RingCat.{u}}
95	  [Functor.IsContinuous G K K'] [Functor.IsContinuous (F ⋙ G) J K']
96	  (ψ : R ⟶ (G.sheafPushforwardContinuous RingCat.{u} K K').obj R')
97	
98	#adaptation_note /-- After nightly-2026-02-23 we need this to avoid timeouts. -/
99	/-- The composition of two pushforward functors on categories of sheaves of modules
100	identify to the pushforward for the composition. -/
101	noncomputable def pushforwardComp :
102	    pushforward.{v} ψ ⋙ pushforward.{v} φ ≅
103	      pushforward.{v} (F := F ⋙ G) (φ ≫ (F.sheafPushforwardContinuous RingCat.{u} J K).map ψ) :=
104	  Iso.refl _
105	
106	-- Not a simp because the type of the LHS is dsimp-able
107	lemma pushforwardComp_hom_app_val_app (M U x) :
108	  ((pushforwardComp φ ψ).hom.app M).val.app U x = x := rfl
109	
110	-- Not a simp because the type of the LHS is dsimp-able
111	lemma pushforwardComp_inv_app_val_app (M U x) :
112	  ((pushforwardComp φ ψ).inv.app M).val.app U x = x := rfl
113	
114	variable {G' : D' ⥤ D''} {R'' : Sheaf K'' RingCat.{u}}
115	  [Functor.IsContinuous G' K' K'']
116	  [Functor.IsContinuous (G ⋙ G') K K'']
117	  [Functor.IsContinuous ((F ⋙ G) ⋙ G') J K'']
118	  [Functor.IsContinuous (F ⋙ G ⋙ G') J K'']
119	  (ψ' : R' ⟶ (G'.sheafPushforwardContinuous RingCat.{u} K' K'').obj R'')
120	
121	lemma pushforward_assoc :
122	    (pushforward ψ').isoWhiskerLeft (pushforwardComp φ ψ) ≪≫
123	      pushforwardComp (F := F ⋙ G)
124	        (φ ≫ (F.sheafPushforwardContinuous RingCat.{u} J K).map ψ) ψ' =
125	    ((pushforward ψ').associator (pushforward ψ) (pushforward φ)).symm ≪≫
126	      isoWhiskerRight (pushforwardComp ψ ψ') (pushforward φ) ≪≫
127	      pushforwardComp (G := G ⋙ G') φ (ψ ≫
128	        (G.sheafPushforwardContinuous RingCat.{u} K K').map ψ') := by ext; rfl
129	
130	end
131	
132	lemma pushforward_comp_id :
133	    pushforwardComp.{v} (F := 𝟭 C) (𝟙 S) φ =
134	      isoWhiskerLeft (pushforward.{v} φ) (pushforwardId S) ≪≫ rightUnitor _ := by ext; rfl
135	
136	lemma pushforward_id_comp :
137	    pushforwardComp.{v} (G := 𝟭 _) φ (𝟙 R) =
138	      isoWhiskerRight (pushforwardId R) (pushforward.{v} φ) ≪≫ leftUnitor _ := by ext; rfl
139	
140	end
141	
142	section NatTrans
143	
144	variable {C : Type u₁} [Category.{v₁} C] {D : Type u₂} [Category.{v₂} D]
145	  {J : GrothendieckTopology C} {K : GrothendieckTopology D}
146	  {F G H : C ⥤ D} {T : Sheaf J RingCat.{u}} {S : Sheaf K RingCat.{u}}
147	  [Functor.IsContinuous F J K]
148	  [Functor.IsContinuous G J K]
149	  [Functor.IsContinuous H J K]
150	  (φ : T ⟶ (G.sheafPushforwardContinuous RingCat.{u} J K).obj S)
151	
152	/-- A natural transformation gives a natural transformation between the pushforward functors. -/
153	noncomputable
154	def pushforwardNatTrans (α : F ⟶ G) :
155	    pushforward.{v} φ ⟶
156	      pushforward.{v} (φ ≫ (Functor.sheafPushforwardContinuousNatTrans α _ _ _).app S) where
157	  app X :=
158	  { val.app U := (ModuleCat.restrictScalars (φ.hom.app U).hom).map (X.val.map (α.app U.unop).op)
159	    val.naturality {U V} i := by
160	      ext x
161	      dsimp
162	      change (X.val.presheaf.map (G.map i.unop).op ≫ X.val.presheaf.map (α.app V.unop).op) _ =
163	        (X.val.presheaf.map (α.app U.unop).op ≫ X.val.presheaf.map (F.map i.unop).op) _
164	      simp only [← CategoryTheory.Functor.map_comp, ← op_comp, α.naturality] }
165	  naturality {X Y} f := by
166	    ext U x
167	    exact congr($(f.val.naturality (α.app U.unop).op) x).symm
168	
169	@[simp] lemma pushforwardNatTrans_app_val_app (α : F ⟶ G) (M U x) :
170	    ((pushforwardNatTrans φ α).app M).val.app U x = M.val.map (α.app U.unop).op x := rfl
171	
172	@[simp]
173	lemma pushforwardNatTrans_id :
174	    pushforwardNatTrans φ (𝟙 G) = (pushforwardCongr (by cat_disch)).hom := by cat_disch
175	
176	@[simp]
177	lemma pushforwardNatTrans_comp (α : F ⟶ G) (β : G ⟶ H)
178	    (φ : T ⟶ (H.sheafPushforwardContinuous RingCat.{u} J K).obj S) :
179	    pushforwardNatTrans φ (α ≫ β) = pushforwardNatTrans φ β ≫ pushforwardNatTrans _ α ≫
180	      (pushforwardCongr (by cat_disch)).hom := by cat_disch
181	
182	@[simp]
183	lemma pushforwardNatTrans_app_val_app_apply (α : F ⟶ G) (X U x) :
184	    ((pushforwardNatTrans φ α).app X).val.app U x = X.val.map (α.app U.unop).op x := rfl
185	
186	/-- A natural isomorphism gives a natural isomorphism between the pushforward functors. -/
187	@[simps]
188	noncomputable def pushforwardNatIso (α : F ≅ G) :
189	    pushforward.{v} φ ≅
190	      pushforward.{v} (φ ≫ (Functor.sheafPushforwardContinuousNatTrans α.hom _ _ _).app S) where
191	  hom := pushforwardNatTrans _ α.hom
192	  inv := pushforwardNatTrans _ α.inv ≫
193	    (pushforwardCongr (by ext : 3; simp [← Functor.map_comp, ← op_comp])).hom
194	  hom_inv_id := by
195	    ext X U x
196	    suffices X.val.presheaf.map (α.hom.app U.unop).op ≫
197	      X.val.presheaf.map (α.inv.app U.unop).op = 𝟙 _ from congr($this x)
198	    simp only [← Functor.map_comp, ← op_comp,
199	      Iso.inv_hom_id_app, op_id, CategoryTheory.Functor.map_id]
200	  inv_hom_id := by
201	    ext X U x
202	    suffices X.val.presheaf.map (α.inv.app U.unop).op ≫
203	      X.val.presheaf.map (α.hom.app U.unop).op = 𝟙 _ from congr($this x)
204	    simp only [← Functor.map_comp, ← op_comp,
205	      Iso.hom_inv_id_app, op_id, CategoryTheory.Functor.map_id]
206	
207	end NatTrans
208	
209	section Adjunction
210	
211	variable {C : Type u₁} [Category.{v₁} C] {D : Type u₂} [Category.{v₂} D]
212	  {J : GrothendieckTopology C} {K : GrothendieckTopology D} {F : C ⥤ D} {G : D ⥤ C}
213	  {S : Sheaf J RingCat.{u}} {R : Sheaf K RingCat.{u}}
214	  [Functor.IsContinuous F J K]
215	  [Functor.IsContinuous G K J]
216	  (adj : F ⊣ G)
217	  (φ : S ⟶ (F.sheafPushforwardContinuous RingCat.{u} J K).obj R)
218	  (ψ : R ⟶ (G.sheafPushforwardContinuous RingCat.{u} K J).obj S)
219	  (H₁ : Functor.whiskerRight (NatTrans.op adj.counit) R.obj = ψ.hom ≫ G.op.whiskerLeft φ.hom)
220	  (H₂ : φ.hom ≫ F.op.whiskerLeft ψ.hom ≫
221	    Functor.whiskerRight (NatTrans.op adj.unit) S.obj = 𝟙 S.obj)
222	
223	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
224	/-- If `F ⊣ G`, then the pushforwards along `F` and `G` are also adjoint. -/
225	noncomputable
226	def pushforwardPushforwardAdj : pushforward.{v} φ ⊣ pushforward.{v} ψ where
227	  unit :=
228	    letI := CategoryTheory.Functor.isContinuous_comp G F K J K
229	    (pushforwardId _).inv ≫ pushforwardNatTrans (𝟙 _) adj.counit ≫
230	      (pushforwardCongr (by ext1; simpa)).hom ≫ (pushforwardComp _ _).inv
231	  counit :=
232	    letI := CategoryTheory.Functor.isContinuous_comp F G J K J
233	    (pushforwardComp _ _).hom ≫ pushforwardNatTrans _ adj.unit ≫
234	      (pushforwardCongr (by ext1; simpa)).hom ≫ (pushforwardId _).hom
235	  left_triangle_components X := by
236	    ext U x
237	    change (X.val.presheaf.map (adj.counit.app (F.obj U.unop)).op ≫
238	      X.val.presheaf.map (F.map (adj.unit.app U.unop)).op) _ = _
239	    dsimp only [id_obj]
240	    rw [← Functor.map_comp, ← op_comp, adj.left_triangle_components]
241	    simp
242	  right_triangle_components X := by
243	    ext U x
244	    change (X.val.presheaf.map (G.map (adj.counit.app U.unop)).op ≫
245	      X.val.presheaf.map (adj.unit.app (G.obj U.unop)).op) _ = _
246	    rw [← Functor.map_comp, ← op_comp, adj.right_triangle_components]
247	    simp
248	
249	-- Not a simp because the type of the LHS is dsimp-able
250	lemma pushforwardPushforwardAdj_unit_app_val_app (M U x) :
251	    ((pushforwardPushforwardAdj adj φ ψ H₁ H₂).unit.app M).val.app U x =
252	      M.val.map (adj.counit.app U.unop).op x := rfl
253	
254	-- Not a simp because the type of the LHS is dsimp-able
255	lemma pushforwardPushforwardAdj_counit_app_val_app (M U x) :
256	    ((pushforwardPushforwardAdj adj φ ψ H₁ H₂).counit.app M).val.app U x =
257	      M.val.map (adj.unit.app U.unop).op x := rfl
258	
259	noncomputable section
260	
261	open CategoryTheory Limits
262	
263	variable {C : Type u'} [Category.{v'} C] [HasBinaryProducts C] {J : GrothendieckTopology C}
264	  {R : Sheaf J RingCat.{u}}
265	
266	/-- The canonical morphism from `R` to the pushforward of its restriction to `Over x`. -/
267	def pushforwardOver (x : C) :
268	    R ⟶ ((Over.star x).sheafPushforwardContinuous RingCat J (J.over x)).obj (R.over x) :=
269	  ⟨{app U := R.obj.map Limits.prod.snd.op
270	    naturality U V f := by simp [← Functor.map_comp, ← op_comp]; rfl }⟩
271	
272	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
273	/-- The adjunction between restriction to `Over x` and pushforward along `Over.star x`. -/
274	def overPushforwardOverAdj (x : C) :
275	    pushforward.{w} (𝟙 (R.over x)) ⊣ pushforward.{w} (pushforwardOver x) := by
276	  refine pushforwardPushforwardAdj (Over.forgetAdjStar x) (𝟙 (R.over x)) _ ?_ ?_
277	  · ext y : 2
278	    simp [pushforwardOver]
279	  · ext y : 2
280	    simp [pushforwardOver, ← Functor.map_comp, ← op_comp]
281	
282	instance (x : C) : IsLeftAdjoint (pushforward.{w} (𝟙 (R.over x))) where
283	  exists_rightAdjoint := ⟨_, Nonempty.intro (overPushforwardOverAdj x)⟩
284	
285	end
286	
287	end Adjunction
288	
289	section Equivalence
290	
291	variable {C : Type u₁} [Category.{v₁} C] {D : Type u₂} [Category.{v₂} D]
292	  {J : GrothendieckTopology C} {K : GrothendieckTopology D} (eqv : C ≌ D)
293	  {S : Sheaf J RingCat.{u}} {R : Sheaf K RingCat.{u}}
294	  [Functor.IsContinuous eqv.functor J K]
295	  [Functor.IsContinuous eqv.inverse K J]
296	  (φ : S ⟶ (eqv.functor.sheafPushforwardContinuous RingCat.{u} J K).obj R)
297	  (ψ : R ⟶ (eqv.inverse.sheafPushforwardContinuous RingCat.{u} K J).obj S)
298	  (H₁ : Functor.whiskerRight (NatTrans.op eqv.counit) R.obj =
299	    ψ.hom ≫ eqv.inverse.op.whiskerLeft φ.hom)
300	  (H₂ : φ.hom ≫ eqv.functor.op.whiskerLeft ψ.hom ≫
301	    Functor.whiskerRight (NatTrans.op eqv.unit) S.obj = 𝟙 S.obj)
302	
303	/-- If `e : C ≌ D`, then the pushforwards along `e.functor` and `e.inverse` forms an equivalence. -/
304	noncomputable
305	def pushforwardPushforwardEquivalence : SheafOfModules R ≌ SheafOfModules S where
306	  functor := pushforward.{v} φ
307	  inverse := pushforward.{v} ψ
308	  unitIso :=
309	    letI := CategoryTheory.Functor.isContinuous_comp eqv.inverse eqv.functor K J K
310	    (pushforwardId _).symm ≪≫ pushforwardNatIso _ eqv.counitIso ≪≫
311	      pushforwardCongr (by ext1; simpa) ≪≫ (pushforwardComp _ _).symm
312	  counitIso :=
313	    letI := CategoryTheory.Functor.isContinuous_comp eqv.functor eqv.inverse J K J
314	    pushforwardComp _ _ ≪≫ pushforwardNatIso _ eqv.unitIso ≪≫
315	      pushforwardCongr (by ext1; simpa) ≪≫ pushforwardId _
316	  functor_unitIso_comp :=
317	    (pushforwardPushforwardAdj eqv.toAdjunction φ ψ H₁ H₂).left_triangle_components
318	
319	-- Not a simp because the type of the LHS is dsimp-able
320	lemma pushforwardPushforwardEquivalence_unit_app_val_app (M U x) :
321	    ((pushforwardPushforwardEquivalence eqv φ ψ H₁ H₂).unit.app M).val.app U x =
322	      M.val.map (eqv.counit.app U.unop).op x := rfl
323	
324	-- Not a simp because the type of the LHS is dsimp-able
325	lemma pushforwardPushforwardEquivalence_counit_app_val_app (M U x) :
326	    ((pushforwardPushforwardEquivalence eqv φ ψ H₁ H₂).counit.app M).val.app U x =
327	      M.val.map (eqv.unit.app U.unop).op x := rfl
328	
329	end Equivalence
330	
331	end SheafOfModules

17	/-!
18	# The category of sheaves of modules over a scheme
19	
20	In this file, we define the abelian category of sheaves of modules
21	`X.Modules` over a scheme `X`, and study its basic functoriality.
22	
23	-/
24	
25	@[expose] public section
26	
27	universe t u
28	
29	open CategoryTheory Limits TopologicalSpace SheafOfModules Bicategory
30	
31	namespace AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme
32	
33	variable {X Y Z T : Scheme.{u}}
34	
35	variable (X) in
36	/-- The category of sheaves of modules over a scheme. -/
37	def Modules := SheafOfModules.{u} X.ringCatSheaf
38	
39	namespace Modules
40	
41	/-- Morphisms between `𝒪ₓ`-modules. Use `Hom.app` to act on sections. -/
42	def Hom (M N : X.Modules) : Type u := SheafOfModules.Hom M N
43	
44	instance : Category X.Modules where
45	  Hom := Modules.Hom
46	  __ := (inferInstance : Category (SheafOfModules.{u} X.ringCatSheaf))
47	
48	noncomputable instance : Abelian X.Modules :=
49	  inferInstanceAs <| Abelian (SheafOfModules.{u} X.ringCatSheaf)
50	instance : HasLimits X.Modules := inferInstanceAs (HasLimits (SheafOfModules X.ringCatSheaf))
51	instance : HasColimits X.Modules := inferInstanceAs (HasColimits (SheafOfModules X.ringCatSheaf))
52	
53	section Functor
54	
55	variable (X) in
56	/-- The forgetful functor from `𝒪ₓ`-modules to presheaves of modules.
57	This is mostly useful to transport results from (pre)sheaves of modules to `𝒪ₓ`-modules and
58	usually shouldn't be used directly when working with actual `𝒪ₓ`-modules. -/
59	def toPresheafOfModules : X.Modules ⥤ X.PresheafOfModules := SheafOfModules.forget _
60	
61	/-- The forgetful functor from `𝒪ₓ`-modules to presheaves of modules is fully faithful. -/
62	def fullyFaithfulToPresheafOfModules : (Modules.toPresheafOfModules X).FullyFaithful :=
63	  SheafOfModules.fullyFaithfulForget _
64	
65	instance : (toPresheafOfModules X).Full := fullyFaithfulToPresheafOfModules.full
66	instance : (toPresheafOfModules X).Faithful := fullyFaithfulToPresheafOfModules.faithful
67	instance : (toPresheafOfModules X).IsRightAdjoint :=
68	  (PresheafOfModules.sheafificationAdjunction (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.obj)).isRightAdjoint
69	
70	variable (X) in
71	/-- The forgetful functor from `𝒪ₓ`-modules to presheaves of abelian groups. -/
72	noncomputable def toPresheaf : X.Modules ⥤ TopCat.Presheaf Ab X :=
73	  toPresheafOfModules X ⋙ PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf _
74	
75	instance : (toPresheaf X).Faithful := .comp _ (PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf _)
76	instance : PreservesLimits (toPresheaf X) := comp_preservesLimits _ (PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf _)
77	instance : (toPresheaf X).ReflectsIsomorphisms :=
78	  reflectsIsomorphisms_comp _ (PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf _)
79	
80	end Functor
81	
82	variable {M N K : X.Modules} {φ : M ⟶ N} {U V : X.Opens}
83	
84	section Presheaf
85	
86	/-- The underlying abelian presheaf of an `𝒪ₓ`-module. -/
87	noncomputable def presheaf (M : X.Modules) : TopCat.Presheaf Ab X := M.1.presheaf
88	
89	/-- Notation for sections of a presheaf of module. -/
90	scoped[AlgebraicGeometry] notation3 "Γ(" M ", " U ")" => (Scheme.Modules.presheaf M).obj (.op U)
91	
92	instance : Module Γ(X, U) Γ(M, U) := (M.val.obj (.op U)).isModule
93	
94	variable (M) in
95	@[simp] lemma map_smul (i : U ⟶ V) (r : Γ(X, V)) (x : Γ(M, V)) :
96	    M.presheaf.map i.op (r • x) = X.presheaf.map i.op r • M.presheaf.map i.op x :=
97	  M.val.map_smul _ _ _
98	
99	/-- The underlying map between abelian presheaves of a morphism of `𝒪ₓ`-modules. -/
100	noncomputable def Hom.mapPresheaf (φ : M ⟶ N) : M.presheaf ⟶ N.presheaf :=
101	  (toPresheaf X).map φ
102	
103	/-- The application of a morphism of `𝒪ₓ`-modules to sections. -/
104	def Hom.app (φ : M ⟶ N) (U : X.Opens) : Γ(M, U) ⟶ Γ(N, U) :=
105	  (forget₂ _ _).map (φ.val.app (.op U))
106	
107	@[simp] lemma mapPresheaf_app (φ : M ⟶ N) (U) : φ.mapPresheaf.app U = φ.app U.unop := rfl
108	
109	@[simp]
110	lemma Hom.app_smul (φ : M ⟶ N) (r : Γ(X, U)) (x : Γ(M, U)) :
111	    φ.app U (r • x) = r • φ.app U x :=
112	  (φ.val.app (.op U)).hom.map_smul r x
113	
114	@[simp] lemma Hom.add_app (φ ψ : M ⟶ N) : (φ + ψ).app U = φ.app U + ψ.app U := rfl
115	@[simp] lemma Hom.sub_app (φ ψ : M ⟶ N) : (φ - ψ).app U = φ.app U - ψ.app U := rfl
116	@[simp] lemma Hom.zero_app : (0 : M ⟶ N).app U = 0 := rfl
117	@[simp] lemma Hom.id_app (M : X.Modules) : (𝟙 M :).app U = 𝟙 _ := rfl
118	@[simp] lemma Hom.comp_app (φ : M ⟶ N) (ψ : N ⟶ K) : (φ ≫ ψ).app U = φ.app U ≫ ψ.app U := rfl
119	
120	@[ext]
121	lemma hom_ext (f g : M ⟶ N) (H : ∀ U, f.app U = g.app U) : f = g := by
122	  apply SheafOfModules.hom_ext
123	  ext U x
124	  exact congr($(H U.unop) x)
125	
126	lemma isSheaf (M : X.Modules) : M.presheaf.IsSheaf := SheafOfModules.isSheaf M
127	
128	@[simp] lemma toPresheaf_obj : (toPresheaf X).obj M = M.presheaf := rfl
129	@[simp] lemma toPresheaf_map : (toPresheaf X).map φ = φ.mapPresheaf := rfl
130	
131	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
132	lemma Hom.isIso_iff_isIso_app {M N : X.Modules} {φ : M ⟶ N} :
133	    IsIso φ ↔ ∀ U, IsIso (φ.app U) := by
134	  rw [← isIso_iff_of_reflects_iso _ (toPresheaf X), NatTrans.isIso_iff_isIso_app]
135	  simp [Opposite.op_surjective.forall]
136	
137	instance [IsIso φ] : IsIso (φ.app U) := Hom.isIso_iff_isIso_app.mp ‹_› _
138	
139	@[simp, push ←]
140	lemma inv_app [IsIso φ] : (inv φ).app U = inv (φ.app U) := by
141	  apply IsIso.eq_inv_of_hom_inv_id
142	  simp [← Hom.comp_app]
143	
144	end Presheaf
145	
146	noncomputable section Functorial
147	
148	variable (f : X ⟶ Y) (g : Y ⟶ Z) (h : Z ⟶ T)
149	
150	/-- The pushforward functor for categories of sheaves of modules over schemes. -/
151	def pushforward : X.Modules ⥤ Y.Modules :=
152	  SheafOfModules.pushforward f.toRingCatSheafHom
153	
154	@[simp]
155	lemma pushforward_obj_obj (M : X.Modules) (U : Y.Opens) :
156	    Γ((pushforward f).obj M, U) = Γ(M, f ⁻¹ᵁ U) := rfl
157	
158	@[simp]
159	lemma pushforward_obj_presheaf_map {U V : Y.Opens} (i : U ⟶ V) :
160	    ((pushforward f).obj M).presheaf.map i.op = M.presheaf.map ((Opens.map f.base).map i).op := rfl
161	
162	@[simp]
163	lemma pushforward_map_app (φ : M ⟶ N) (U : Y.Opens) :
164	    ((pushforward f).map φ).app U = φ.app (f ⁻¹ᵁ U) := rfl
165	
166	/-- The pullback functor for categories of sheaves of modules over schemes. -/
167	def pullback : Y.Modules ⥤ X.Modules :=
168	  SheafOfModules.pullback f.toRingCatSheafHom
169	
170	/-- The pullback functor for categories of sheaves of modules over schemes
171	is left adjoint to the pushforward functor. -/
172	def pullbackPushforwardAdjunction : pullback f ⊣ pushforward f :=
173	  SheafOfModules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction _
174	
175	section
176	
177	attribute [local instance] preservesBinaryBiproducts_of_preservesBinaryCoproducts
178	  preservesBinaryBiproducts_of_preservesBinaryProducts
179	
180	instance : (pullback f).IsLeftAdjoint := (pullbackPushforwardAdjunction f).isLeftAdjoint
181	instance : (pushforward f).IsRightAdjoint := (pullbackPushforwardAdjunction f).isRightAdjoint
182	instance : (pushforward f).Additive := Functor.additive_of_preservesBinaryBiproducts _
183	instance : (pullback f).Additive := Functor.additive_of_preservesBinaryBiproducts _
184	
185	end
186	
187	variable (X) in
188	/-- The pushforward of sheaves of modules by the identity morphism identifies
189	to the identity functor. -/
190	def pushforwardId : pushforward (𝟙 X) ≅ 𝟭 _ :=
191	  SheafOfModules.pushforwardId _
192	
193	@[simp] lemma pushforwardId_hom_app_app : ((pushforwardId X).hom.app M).app U = 𝟙 _ := rfl
194	@[simp] lemma pushforwardId_inv_app_app : ((pushforwardId X).inv.app M).app U = 𝟙 _ := rfl
195	
196	variable (X) in
197	/-- The pullback of sheaves of modules by the identity morphism identifies
198	to the identity functor. -/
199	def pullbackId : pullback (𝟙 X) ≅ 𝟭 _ :=
200	  SheafOfModules.pullbackId _
201	
202	variable (X) in
203	lemma conjugateEquiv_pullbackId_hom :
204	    conjugateEquiv .id (pullbackPushforwardAdjunction (𝟙 X)) (pullbackId X).hom =
205	      (pushforwardId X).inv :=
206	  SheafOfModules.conjugateEquiv_pullbackId_hom _
207	
208	/-- The composition of two pushforward functors for sheaves of modules on schemes
209	identify to the pushforward for the composition. -/
210	def pushforwardComp :
211	    pushforward f ⋙ pushforward g ≅ pushforward (f ≫ g) :=
212	  SheafOfModules.pushforwardComp _ _
213	
214	@[simp] lemma pushforwardComp_hom_app_app (U) : ((pushforwardComp f g).hom.app M).app U = 𝟙 _ := rfl
215	@[simp] lemma pushforwardComp_inv_app_app (U) : ((pushforwardComp f g).inv.app M).app U = 𝟙 _ := rfl
216	
217	/-- The composition of two pullback functors for sheaves of modules on schemes
218	identify to the pullback for the composition. -/
219	def pullbackComp :
220	    pullback g ⋙ pullback f ≅ pullback (f ≫ g) :=
221	  SheafOfModules.pullbackComp _ _
222	
223	/-- Pushforwards along equal morphisms are isomorphic. -/
224	def pushforwardCongr {f g : X ⟶ Y} (hf : f = g) : pushforward f ≅ pushforward g :=
225	    pushforwardNatIso _ (Opens.mapIso _ _ (hf ▸ rfl)) ≪≫
226	      SheafOfModules.pushforwardCongr (by cat_disch)
227	
228	@[simp] lemma pushforwardCongr_hom_app_app {f g : X ⟶ Y} (hf : f = g) (U : Y.Opens) :
229	    ((pushforwardCongr hf).hom.app M).app U = M.presheaf.map (eqToHom (hf ▸ rfl)).op := rfl
230	
231	@[simp] lemma pushforwardCongr_inv_app_app {f g : X ⟶ Y} (hf : f = g) (U : Y.Opens) :
232	    ((pushforwardCongr hf).inv.app M).app U = M.presheaf.map (eqToHom (hf ▸ rfl)).op := rfl
233	
234	/-- Inverse images along equal morphisms are isomorphic. -/
235	def pullbackCongr {f g : X ⟶ Y} (hf : f = g) : pullback f ≅ pullback g :=
236	  eqToIso (hf ▸ rfl)
237	
238	lemma conjugateEquiv_pullbackComp_inv :
239	    conjugateEquiv ((pullbackPushforwardAdjunction g).comp (pullbackPushforwardAdjunction f))
240	      (pullbackPushforwardAdjunction (f ≫ g)) (pullbackComp f g).inv =
241	    (pushforwardComp f g).hom :=
242	  SheafOfModules.conjugateEquiv_pullbackComp_inv _ _
243	
244	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
245	@[reassoc]
246	lemma pseudofunctor_associativity :
247	    (pullbackComp f (g ≫ h)).inv ≫
248	      Functor.whiskerRight (pullbackComp g h).inv _ ≫ (Functor.associator _ _ _).hom ≫
249	        Functor.whiskerLeft _ (pullbackComp f g).hom ≫ (pullbackComp (f ≫ g) h).hom =
250	    eqToHom (by simp) := by
251	  let e₁ := pullbackComp f (g ≫ h)
252	  let e₂ := Functor.isoWhiskerRight (pullbackComp g h) (pullback f)
253	  let e₃ := Functor.isoWhiskerLeft (pullback h) (pullbackComp f g)
254	  let e₄ := pullbackComp (f ≫ g) h
255	  change e₁.inv ≫ e₂.inv ≫ (Functor.associator _ _ _).hom ≫ e₃.hom ≫ e₄.hom = _
256	  have : e₃.hom ≫ e₄.hom = (Functor.associator _ _ _).inv ≫ e₂.hom ≫ e₁.hom :=
257	    congr_arg Iso.hom (SheafOfModules.pullback_assoc.{u}
258	      h.toRingCatSheafHom g.toRingCatSheafHom f.toRingCatSheafHom)
259	  simp [this]
260	
261	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
262	@[reassoc]
263	lemma pseudofunctor_left_unitality :
264	    (pullbackComp f (𝟙 Y)).inv ≫
265	      Functor.whiskerRight (pullbackId Y).hom (pullback f) ≫ (Functor.leftUnitor _).hom =
266	        eqToHom (by simp) := by
267	  let e₁ := pullbackComp f (𝟙 _)
268	  let e₂ := Functor.isoWhiskerRight (pullbackId Y) (pullback f)
269	  let e₃ := (pullback f).leftUnitor
270	  change e₁.inv ≫ e₂.hom ≫ e₃.hom = _
271	  have : e₁.hom = e₂.hom ≫ e₃.hom :=
272	    congr_arg Iso.hom (SheafOfModules.pullback_id_comp.{u} f.toRingCatSheafHom)
273	  simp [← this]
274	
275	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
276	@[reassoc]
277	lemma pseudofunctor_right_unitality :
278	    (pullbackComp (𝟙 X) f).inv ≫
279	      Functor.whiskerLeft (pullback f) (pullbackId X).hom ≫ (Functor.rightUnitor _).hom =
280	        eqToHom (by simp) := by
281	  let e₁ := pullbackComp (𝟙 _) f
282	  let e₂ := Functor.isoWhiskerLeft (pullback f) (pullbackId _)
283	  let e₃ := (pullback f).rightUnitor
284	  change e₁.inv ≫ e₂.hom ≫ e₃.hom = _
285	  have : e₁.hom = e₂.hom ≫ e₃.hom :=
286	    congr_arg Iso.hom (SheafOfModules.pullback_comp_id.{u} f.toRingCatSheafHom)
287	  simp [← this]
288	
289	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
290	attribute [local simp] pseudofunctor_associativity pseudofunctor_left_unitality
291	  pseudofunctor_right_unitality Bicategory.toNatTrans_conjugateEquiv
292	  conjugateEquiv_pullbackId_hom Adjunction.ofCat_comp conjugateEquiv_pullbackComp_inv in
293	/-- The pseudofunctor from `Schemeᵒᵖ` to the bicategory of adjunctions which sends
294	a scheme `X` to the category `X.Modules` of sheaves of modules over `X`.
295	(This contains both the covariant and the contravariant functorialities of
296	these categories.) -/
297	@[simps! obj_obj map_l map_r map_adj
298	  mapId_hom_τl mapId_hom_τr mapId_inv_τl mapId_inv_τr
299	  mapComp_hom_τl mapComp_hom_τr mapComp_inv_τl mapComp_inv_τr]
300	def pseudofunctor :
301	    Pseudofunctor (LocallyDiscrete Scheme.{u}ᵒᵖ) (Adj Cat) :=
302	  LocallyDiscrete.mkPseudofunctor
303	    (fun X ↦ Adj.mk (Cat.of X.unop.Modules))
304	    (fun f ↦ .mk (pullbackPushforwardAdjunction f.unop).toCat)
305	    (fun _ ↦ Adj.iso₂Mk (Cat.Hom.isoMk (pullbackId _))
306	        (Cat.Hom.isoMk (pushforwardId _).symm))
307	    (fun _ _ ↦ Adj.iso₂Mk (Cat.Hom.isoMk (pullbackComp _ _).symm)
308	        (Cat.Hom.isoMk (pushforwardComp _ _)))
309	
310	end Functorial
311	
312	noncomputable section Restriction
313	
314	variable (f : X ⟶ Y) (g : Y ⟶ Z) [IsOpenImmersion f] [IsOpenImmersion g]
315	
316	/-- Restriction of an `𝒪ₓ`-module along an open immersion.
317	This is isomorphic to the pullback functor (see `restrictFunctorIsoPullback`)
318	but has better defeqs. -/
319	def restrictFunctor : Y.Modules ⥤ X.Modules :=
320	  letI α : X.presheaf ⟶ f.opensFunctor.op ⋙ Y.presheaf := { app U := (f.appIso U.unop).inv }
321	  SheafOfModules.pushforward (F := f.opensFunctor)
322	    ⟨Functor.whiskerRight α (forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)⟩
323	
324	/-- The restriction of a module along an open immersion. -/
325	abbrev restrict (M : Y.Modules) (f : X ⟶ Y) [IsOpenImmersion f] : X.Modules :=
326	  (restrictFunctor f).obj M
327	
328	@[simp] lemma restrict_obj (M : Y.Modules) (f : X ⟶ Y) [IsOpenImmersion f] (U) :
329	    Γ(M.restrict f, U) = Γ(M, f ''ᵁ U) := rfl
330	
331	@[simp] lemma restrict_map (M : Y.Modules) (f : X ⟶ Y) [IsOpenImmersion f] {U V} (i : U ⟶ V) :
332	    (M.restrict f).presheaf.map i.op = M.presheaf.map (f.opensFunctor.map i).op := rfl
333	
334	/-- The restriction of a module along an open immersion. -/
335	def restrictFunctorAdjCounitIso : pushforward f ⋙ restrictFunctor f ≅ 𝟭 _ :=
336	  letI := CategoryTheory.Functor.isContinuous_comp.{u} f.opensFunctor (Opens.map f.base)
337	    (Opens.grothendieckTopology X) (Opens.grothendieckTopology Y) (Opens.grothendieckTopology X)
338	  (SheafOfModules.pushforwardComp _ _) ≪≫ pushforwardNatIso _ (NatIso.ofComponents
339	      (fun U ↦ eqToIso (f.preimage_image_eq U).symm) fun _ ↦ rfl) ≪≫
340	    SheafOfModules.pushforwardCongr (by ext U x; exact
341	      congr($(f.appIso_inv_app_presheafMap U.unop) x)) ≪≫ SheafOfModules.pushforwardId _
342	
343	/-- Restriction is right adjoint to pushforward. -/
344	def restrictAdjunction : restrictFunctor f ⊣ pushforward f := by
345	  refine pushforwardPushforwardAdj (by exact f.isOpenEmbedding.isOpenMap.adjunction) _ _ ?_ ?_
346	  · ext U x; exact congr($((f.app_appIso_inv _).symm).hom x)
347	  · ext U x
348	    have : (f.appIso U.unop).inv ≫ f.app _ ≫
349	      X.presheaf.map (eqToHom (f.preimage_image_eq U.unop).symm).op = 𝟙 _ := by
350	      rw [Scheme.Hom.appIso_inv_app_assoc, ← Functor.map_comp, ← X.presheaf.map_id]; rfl
351	    exact congr($this x)
352	
353	instance : IsIso (restrictAdjunction f).counit :=
354	  inferInstanceAs (IsIso <| (restrictFunctorAdjCounitIso f).hom)
355	
356	instance : (restrictFunctor f).IsLeftAdjoint := (restrictAdjunction f).isLeftAdjoint
357	instance : (pushforward f).Full := (restrictAdjunction f).fullyFaithfulROfIsIsoCounit.full
358	instance : (pushforward f).Faithful := (restrictAdjunction f).fullyFaithfulROfIsIsoCounit.faithful
359	
360	@[simp]
361	lemma restrictAdjunction_unit_app_app (M : Y.Modules) (U : Y.Opens) :
362	    ((restrictAdjunction f).unit.app M).app U =
363	      M.presheaf.map (homOfLE (f.image_preimage_le U)).op := rfl
364	
365	@[simp]
366	lemma restrictAdjunction_counit_app_app (M : X.Modules) (U : X.Opens) :
367	    ((restrictAdjunction f).counit.app M).app U =
368	      M.presheaf.map (eqToHom (f.preimage_image_eq U).symm).op := rfl
369	
370	/-- Restriction is naturally isomorphic to the inverse image. -/
371	def restrictFunctorIsoPullback : restrictFunctor f ≅ pullback f :=
372	  (restrictAdjunction f).leftAdjointUniq (pullbackPushforwardAdjunction f)
373	
374	/-- Restriction along the identity is isomorphic to the identity. -/
375	def restrictFunctorId : restrictFunctor (𝟙 X) ≅ 𝟭 _ :=
376	  SheafOfModules.pushforwardNatIso _ (NatIso.ofComponents (fun _ ↦ eqToIso (by simp))) ≪≫
377	    SheafOfModules.pushforwardCongr
378	      (by ext : 3; simp [← Functor.map_comp, SheafedSpace.sheaf]) ≪≫
379	    SheafOfModules.pushforwardId _
380	
381	@[simp]
382	lemma restrictFunctorId_hom_app_app :
383	    (restrictFunctorId.hom.app M).app U =
384	      M.presheaf.map (eqToHom (show U = 𝟙 X ''ᵁ U by simp)).op := rfl
385	
386	@[simp]
387	lemma restrictFunctorId_inv_app_app :
388	    (restrictFunctorId.inv.app M).app U =
389	      M.presheaf.map (eqToHom (show 𝟙 X ''ᵁ U = U by simp)).op := rfl
390	
391	/-- Restriction along the composition is isomorphic to the composition of restrictions. -/
392	def restrictFunctorComp : restrictFunctor (f ≫ g) ≅ restrictFunctor g ⋙ restrictFunctor f :=
393	  have : (f.opensFunctor ⋙ g.opensFunctor).IsContinuous
394	      (Opens.grothendieckTopology X) (Opens.grothendieckTopology Z) :=
395	    Functor.isContinuous_comp _ _ _ (Opens.grothendieckTopology _) _
396	  SheafOfModules.pushforwardNatIso _ (NatIso.ofComponents fun _ ↦ eqToIso (by simp)) ≪≫
397	    SheafOfModules.pushforwardCongr (by ext : 3; simp [← Functor.map_comp, SheafedSpace.sheaf]) ≪≫
398	    (SheafOfModules.pushforwardComp _ _).symm
399	
400	@[simp]
401	lemma restrictFunctorComp_hom_app_app (M : Z.Modules) :
402	    ((restrictFunctorComp f g).hom.app M).app U = M.presheaf.map (eqToHom (by simp)).op := rfl
403	
404	@[simp]
405	lemma restrictFunctorComp_inv_app_app (M : Z.Modules) :
406	    ((restrictFunctorComp f g).inv.app M).app U = M.presheaf.map (eqToHom (by simp)).op := rfl
407	
408	/-- Restriction along equal morphisms are isomorphic. -/
409	def restrictFunctorCongr {f g : X ⟶ Y} (hf : f = g) [IsOpenImmersion f] [IsOpenImmersion g] :
410	    restrictFunctor f ≅ restrictFunctor g :=
411	  SheafOfModules.pushforwardNatIso _ (NatIso.ofComponents fun _ ↦ eqToIso (by simp [hf])) ≪≫
412	    SheafOfModules.pushforwardCongr (by ext : 3; subst hf; simp)
413	
414	@[simp]
415	lemma restrictFunctorCongr_hom_app_app {f g : X ⟶ Y} (hf : f = g) [IsOpenImmersion f]
416	    [IsOpenImmersion g] (M : Y.Modules) :
417	    ((restrictFunctorCongr hf).hom.app M).app U = M.presheaf.map (eqToHom (by simp [hf])).op := rfl
418	
419	@[simp]
420	lemma restrictFunctorCongr_inv_app_app {f g : X ⟶ Y} (hf : f = g) [IsOpenImmersion f]
421	    [IsOpenImmersion g] (M : Y.Modules) :
422	    ((restrictFunctorCongr hf).inv.app M).app U = M.presheaf.map (eqToHom (by simp [hf])).op := rfl
423	
424	/-- Restriction along open immersions commutes with taking stalks. -/
425	def restrictStalkNatIso (x : X) :
426	    restrictFunctor f ⋙ toPresheaf _ ⋙ TopCat.Presheaf.stalkFunctor _ x ≅
427	    toPresheaf _ ⋙ TopCat.Presheaf.stalkFunctor _ (f x) :=
428	  haveI := Functor.initial_of_adjunction (f.isOpenEmbedding.adjunctionNhds x)
429	  (toPresheaf _ ⋙ (Functor.whiskeringLeft (OpenNhds (f x))ᵒᵖ Y.Opensᵒᵖ Ab).obj
430	      (OpenNhds.inclusion (f x)).op).isoWhiskerLeft
431	      (Functor.Final.colimIso (f.isOpenEmbedding.functorNhds x).op)
432	
433	@[simp]
434	lemma germ_restrictStalkNatIso_hom_app (x : X) (M : Y.Modules) (hxU : x ∈ U) :
435	    ((restrictFunctor f).obj M).presheaf.germ U _ hxU ≫
436	      (restrictStalkNatIso f x).hom.app M = M.presheaf.germ _ _ (by simpa) :=
437	  haveI := Functor.initial_of_adjunction (f.isOpenEmbedding.adjunctionNhds x)
438	  Functor.Final.ι_colimitIso_hom
439	    (f.isOpenEmbedding.functorNhds x).op
440	    ((OpenNhds.inclusion ((ConcreteCategory.hom f.base) x)).op ⋙ M.presheaf) _
441	
442	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
443	@[simp]
444	lemma germ_restrictStalkNatIso_inv_app (x : X) (M : Y.Modules) (hxU : x ∈ U) :
445	    M.presheaf.germ _ _ (by simpa) ≫ (restrictStalkNatIso f x).inv.app M =
446	      ((restrictFunctor f).obj M).presheaf.germ U _ hxU := by
447	  rw [← germ_restrictStalkNatIso_hom_app f x M hxU, Category.assoc, ← NatTrans.comp_app,
448	    Iso.hom_inv_id]
449	  simp
450	
451	end Restriction
452	
453	end AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules

Now let me read the blueprint chapter for the informal proofs.

1	\chapter{Effective descent: restriction of the glued sheaf to a chart}
2	\label{chap:Picard_GlueDescent}
3	
4	\providecommand{\ShMod}{\mathrm{SheafOfModules}}
5	
6	The gluing primitive \cref{def:scheme_modules_glue} produces, out of a glue datum
7	\(D\) of schemes (index set \(J\), charts \(U_i\), overlaps \(V_{ij}\), overlap
8	immersions \(f_{ij} : V_{ij} \hookrightarrow U_i\), transition isomorphisms
9	\(t_{ij} : V_{ij} \xrightarrow{\sim} V_{ji}\), glued scheme \(X\) with chart
10	immersions \(\iota_i : U_i \hookrightarrow X\)), a sheaf of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules
11	\(\mathcal{M}\) as the descent equalizer
12	\[
13	  \mathcal{M} \;=\; \operatorname{eq}(a, b)
14	    \;\hookrightarrow\; \prod_{i} (\iota_i)_{*}\,\mathcal{M}_i
15	\]
16	of two parallel maps into the overlap product. That construction makes
17	\(\mathcal{M}\) but does \emph{not} yet consume the cocycle conditions (C1)/(C2) on
18	the transition family \((g_{ij})\). This chapter discharges the remaining content of
19	descent: it shows that restricting the glued sheaf back to a chart recovers the
20	input sheaf there. Concretely, the canonical \emph{restriction morphism}
21	\(\iota_i^{*}\mathcal{M} \to \mathcal{M}_i\) -- the adjoint transpose along the chart
22	immersion \(\iota_i\) of the \(i\)-th equalizer projection -- is an isomorphism, and
23	it is here that (C1)/(C2) are used. The engine is that pullback along an open
24	immersion is, up to natural isomorphism, the site-level restriction functor
25	(\cref{lem:modules_restrictFunctorIsoPullback_mathlib}); being a right adjoint it
26	preserves the descent equalizer and the pushforward product, so the chart
27	restriction of \(\mathcal{M}\) is computed factor by factor. The non-formal input is
28	the \emph{overlap base change} \(\beta_{ij}\), which identifies the restricted
29	pushforward factor \(\iota_i^{*}(\iota_j)_{*}\mathcal{M}_j\) with a pushforward of a
30	restriction of \(\mathcal{M}_j\) to the overlap, using the cartesianness of the
31	chart--overlap square.
32	
33	\section{The descent equalizer and its overlap base change}
34	\label{sec:gd_equalizer}
35	
36	\begin{definition}
37	
38	\leanok
39	[The pushforward product carrying the descent equalizer]
40	  \label{def:gr_glue_equalizer}
41	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.glueProd}
42	  \uses{def:scheme_modules_glue}
43	  Let \(D\) be a glue datum and \((\mathcal{M}_i)_{i \in J}\) a family of sheaves of
44	  modules with \(\mathcal{M}_i\) on the chart \(U_i\). Write
45	  \[
46	    P \;=\; \prod_{i} (\iota_i)_{*}\,\mathcal{M}_i
47	    \qquad\text{and}\qquad
48	    Q \;=\; \prod_{(i,j)} (f_{ij} \circ \iota_i)_{*}\,\bigl(f_{ij}^{*}\mathcal{M}_i\bigr)
49	  \]
50	  for the \emph{pushforward product} and the \emph{overlap product} of sheaves of
51	  \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules. The two descent legs of \cref{def:scheme_modules_glue},
52	  \[
53	    P \;\underset{b}{\overset{a}{\rightrightarrows}}\; Q,
54	  \]
55	  are: on the \((i,j)\)-component, leg \(a\) restricts the \(i\)-th chart factor to
56	  the overlap \(V_{ij}\) using the unit of the pullback--pushforward adjunction along
57	  \(f_{ij}\) and the pushforward-composition comparison
58	  \((\iota_i)_{*} \cong (f_{ij} \circ \iota_i)_{*} \circ f_{ij}^{*}\); leg \(b\)
59	  restricts the \(j\)-th chart factor, transports it across the inverse transition
60	  isomorphism \(g_{ij}^{-1}\), and reindexes the immersion via the glue condition
61	  \((t_{ij} \circ f_{ji}) \circ \iota_j = f_{ij} \circ \iota_i\). The glued sheaf
62	  \(\mathcal{M} = \operatorname{eq}(a,b)\) is the equalizer of this parallel pair;
63	  \(P\) is the object it embeds into and \(Q\) the object detecting the overlap
64	  agreement. The product \(P\) is the carrier on which the \(i\)-th projection (and,
65	  through it, the restriction morphism) is defined.
66	\end{definition}
67	
68	\begin{lemma}
69	
70	\leanok
71	[Overlap base change of the chart square (\(\beta_{ij}\))]
72	  \label{lem:glueOverlapBaseChangeIso}
73	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.glueOverlapBaseChangeIso}
74	  \uses{lem:modules_restrictFunctorIsoPullback_mathlib}
75	  For chart indices \(i, j \in J\) there is a natural isomorphism of functors
76	  \(\ShMod(\mathcal{O}_{U_j}) \to \ShMod(\mathcal{O}_{U_i})\),
77	  \[
78	    \beta_{ij} \;:\;
79	      \iota_i^{*}\,(\iota_j)_{*}
80	        \;\xrightarrow{\ \sim\ }\;
81	      (f_{ij})_{*}\,(t_{ij} \circ f_{ji})^{*},
82	  \]
83	  identifying, for any sheaf \(\mathcal{N}\) on the chart \(U_j\), the restriction to
84	  the chart \(U_i\) of the pushforward \((\iota_j)_{*}\mathcal{N}\) with the
85	  pushforward along \(f_{ij}\) of the restriction of \(\mathcal{N}\) to the overlap
86	  \(V_{ij}\) along \(t_{ij} \circ f_{ji}\). Here the chart restriction \(\iota_i^{*}\)
87	  and the overlap restriction \((t_{ij} \circ f_{ji})^{*}\) are realised as the
88	  site-level restriction functors, isomorphic to the geometric pullbacks by
89	  \cref{lem:modules_restrictFunctorIsoPullback_mathlib}.
90	\end{lemma}
91	\begin{proof}
92	  \uses{lem:modules_restrictFunctorIsoPullback_mathlib}
93	  \leanok
94	  The isomorphism is the cartesianness of the chart--overlap square, made site-level.
95	  The square
96	  \[
97	    \begin{array}{ccc}
98	      V_{ij} & \xrightarrow{\ t_{ij} \circ f_{ji}\ } & U_j \\
99	      \ \downarrow{\scriptstyle f_{ij}} & & \downarrow{\scriptstyle \iota_j} \\
100	      U_i & \xrightarrow{\ \ \iota_i\ \ } & X
101	    \end{array}
102	  \]
103	  is cartesian, and its cartesianness is visible already on underlying opens: for an
104	  open \(V \subseteq U_i\),
105	  \[
106	    \iota_j^{-1}\bigl(\iota_i(V)\bigr)
107	      \;=\; (t_{ij} \circ f_{ji})\bigl(f_{ij}^{-1}(V)\bigr),
108	  \]
109	  a point of \(U_j\) lands in the image of \(V\) under \(\iota_i\) precisely when it
110	  comes, via the glue relation, from a point of the overlap lying over \(V\). Hence
111	  the two composite opens functors
112	  \[
113	    (\iota_i)_{!} \circ \iota_j^{-1}
114	      \;=\;
115	    f_{ij}^{-1} \circ (t_{ij} \circ f_{ji})_{!}
116	  \]
117	  agree on objects, and -- morphisms of the opens poset being proof-irrelevant -- as
118	  functors. Both \(\iota_i^{*}(\iota_j)_{*}\) and \((f_{ij})_{*}(t_{ij}\circ
119	  f_{ji})^{*}\) are site-level pushforwards along these two (equal) opens functors, so
120	  the comparison is assembled from the pushforward-composition isomorphisms together
121	  with the natural isomorphism transporting one opens functor to the other:
122	  \[
123	    \beta_{ij}
124	      \;=\;
125	    (\text{pushforwardComp})
126	      \;\circ\;
127	    (\text{pushforward of the opens-functor equality})
128	      \;\circ\;
129	    (\text{pushforwardCongr})
130	      \;\circ\;
131	    (\text{pushforwardComp})^{-1}.
132	  \]
133	  The one substantive coherence to check is that the comparison glued from the
134	  object-level opens equality is natural in the open \(V\): on the section level the
135	  two restriction maps induced by the equal composite functors coincide because the
136	  connecting morphisms are the unique arrows of the opens poset between the identified
137	  opens. This is the pointwise compatibility that completes the \(\mathrm{pushforwardCongr}\)
138	  factor.
139	\end{proof}
140	
141	\section{The restriction morphism and effective descent}
142	\label{sec:gd_descent}
143	
144	\begin{lemma}
145	
146	\leanok
147	[The chart restriction morphism of the glued sheaf]
148	  \label{lem:glueRestrictionHom}
149	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.glueRestrictionHom}
150	  \uses{def:gr_glue_equalizer, def:scheme_modules_glue}
151	  Let \(\mathcal{M}\) be the glued sheaf of \cref{def:scheme_modules_glue}. The
152	  \(i\)-th \emph{projection} of \(\mathcal{M}\),
153	  \[
154	    \pi_i \;:\; \mathcal{M} \longrightarrow (\iota_i)_{*}\,\mathcal{M}_i,
155	  \]
156	  is the equalizer inclusion \(\mathcal{M} \hookrightarrow P\)
157	  (\cref{def:gr_glue_equalizer}) followed by the \(i\)-th product projection. Its
158	  adjoint transpose along the chart immersion \(\iota_i\), under the
159	  pullback--pushforward adjunction \(\iota_i^{*} \dashv (\iota_i)_{*}\), is the
160	  \emph{restriction morphism}
161	  \[
162	    \mathrm{r}_i \;:\; \iota_i^{*}\,\mathcal{M} \longrightarrow \mathcal{M}_i .
163	  \]
164	  Equivalently, \(\mathrm{r}_i\) is the unique morphism whose pushforward, precomposed
165	  with the unit, recovers \(\pi_i\).
166	\end{lemma}
167	\begin{proof}
168	  \uses{def:gr_glue_equalizer, def:scheme_modules_glue}
169	  \leanok
170	  The projection \(\pi_i\) is well-defined by \cref{def:gr_glue_equalizer}: the glued
171	  sheaf is the equalizer \(\operatorname{eq}(a,b) \hookrightarrow P\), and composing
172	  the inclusion with the \(i\)-th projection of the product \(P = \prod_i
173	  (\iota_i)_{*}\mathcal{M}_i\) gives a map to \((\iota_i)_{*}\mathcal{M}_i\). The
174	  restriction morphism is then its image under the natural bijection
175	  \[
176	    \operatorname{Hom}_{\mathcal{O}_X}\bigl(\mathcal{M}, (\iota_i)_{*}\mathcal{M}_i\bigr)
177	      \;\cong\;
178	    \operatorname{Hom}_{\mathcal{O}_{U_i}}\bigl(\iota_i^{*}\mathcal{M}, \mathcal{M}_i\bigr)
179	  \]
180	  supplied by the adjunction \(\iota_i^{*} \dashv (\iota_i)_{*}\). The stated
181	  equivalent description is the unit--counit form of this transpose.
182	\end{proof}
183	
184	\begin{theorem}
185	
186	\leanok
187	[Effective descent: the chart restriction morphism is an isomorphism]
188	  \label{thm:isIso_glueRestrictionHom}
189	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.isIso_glueRestrictionHom}
190	  \uses{lem:glueRestrictionHom, lem:glueOverlapBaseChangeIso,
191	    lem:isLimitPullbackCone_mathlib, lem:eq_of_locally_eq_mathlib}
192	  Assume the transition family \((g_{ij})\) satisfies the cocycle conditions (C1) and
193	  (C2) of \cref{def:scheme_modules_glue}. Then for every chart index \(i\) the
194	  restriction morphism \(\mathrm{r}_i : \iota_i^{*}\mathcal{M} \to \mathcal{M}_i\)
195	  (\cref{lem:glueRestrictionHom}) is an isomorphism of sheaves of
196	  \(\mathcal{O}_{U_i}\)-modules.
197	\end{theorem}
198	\begin{proof}
199	  \uses{lem:glueRestrictionHom, lem:glueOverlapBaseChangeIso,
200	    lem:isLimitPullbackCone_mathlib, lem:eq_of_locally_eq_mathlib}
201	  The chart pullback \(\iota_i^{*}\) is naturally isomorphic to the site-level
202	  restriction functor (\cref{lem:modules_restrictFunctorIsoPullback_mathlib}), which
203	  is a right adjoint; consequently \(\iota_i^{*}\) \emph{preserves limits}. Applying
204	  it to the descent equalizer \(\mathcal{M} = \operatorname{eq}(a,b)\) exhibits
205	  \(\iota_i^{*}\mathcal{M}\) as the equalizer of the restricted legs
206	  \(\iota_i^{*}a, \iota_i^{*}b\), and applying it to the product
207	  \(P = \prod_j (\iota_j)_{*}\mathcal{M}_j\) exhibits \(\iota_i^{*}P\) as the product
208	  \(\prod_j \iota_i^{*}(\iota_j)_{*}\mathcal{M}_j\). The overlap base change
209	  \(\beta_{ij}\) of \cref{lem:glueOverlapBaseChangeIso} then identifies the \(j\)-th
210	  factor \(\iota_i^{*}(\iota_j)_{*}\mathcal{M}_j\) with
211	  \((f_{ij})_{*}\bigl((t_{ij} \circ f_{ji})^{*}\mathcal{M}_j\bigr)\). Under these
212	  identifications the restricted descent data becomes the data attached to the chart
213	  \(U_i\) by the cover \(\{f_{ij}\}\) of \(U_i\), and the \(i\)-th factor (where
214	  \(j = i\)) is, by (C1), \(\mathcal{M}_i\) itself.
215	
216	  We produce an inverse \(\mathrm{s}_i : \mathcal{M}_i \to \iota_i^{*}\mathcal{M}\).
217	  Because \(\iota_i^{*}\mathcal{M}\) is the equalizer of the restricted legs, it
218	  suffices to give a morphism \(\mathcal{M}_i \to \iota_i^{*}P\) -- equivalently a
219	  compatible family of morphisms into the factors -- that equalizes
220	  \(\iota_i^{*}a\) and \(\iota_i^{*}b\). The \(j\)-th component is
221	  \[
222	    \mathcal{M}_i
223	      \xrightarrow{\ \eta_{f_{ij}}\ }
224	    (f_{ij})_{*}\,f_{ij}^{*}\mathcal{M}_i
225	      \xrightarrow{\ (f_{ij})_{*} g_{ij}\ }
226	    (f_{ij})_{*}\,(t_{ij} \circ f_{ji})^{*}\mathcal{M}_j
227	      \xrightarrow{\ \beta_{ij}^{-1}\ }
228	    \iota_i^{*}(\iota_j)_{*}\mathcal{M}_j ,
229	  \]
230	  the unit of the pullback--pushforward adjunction along \(f_{ij}\), followed by the
231	  pushforward of the transition isomorphism \(g_{ij}\), followed by the inverse of the
232	  overlap base change. That this family equalizes the two restricted legs is exactly
233	  the triple-overlap multiplicativity (C2), transported through \(\beta\); and the
234	  composite \(\mathrm{r}_i \circ \mathrm{s}_i = \mathrm{id}_{\mathcal{M}_i}\) reduces,
235	  via the \(j = i\) component, to the self-identity (C1) together with the counit
236	  triangle identity of the adjunction. The reverse composite
237	  \(\mathrm{s}_i \circ \mathrm{r}_i = \mathrm{id}\) holds because both sides are
238	  equalizer maps with the same components into \(P\); two such agree once their
239	  factors agree, which is the separatedness of the sheaf equalizer over the chart
240	  cover. The limit computation underlying these equalities is the sheaf
241	  Mayer--Vietoris pullback square (\cref{lem:isLimitPullbackCone_mathlib}), and the
242	  final identification of sections is the uniqueness-over-a-cover principle
243	  (\cref{lem:eq_of_locally_eq_mathlib}). Hence \(\mathrm{r}_i\) is an isomorphism.
244	\end{proof}
245	
246	\begin{definition}
247	
248	\leanok
249	[The restriction isomorphism of the glued sheaf]
250	  \label{def:glueRestrictionIso}
251	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.glueRestrictionIso}
252	  % NOTE: this is the 1:1 home for the Lean decl `glueRestrictionIso`
253	  % (AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/GlueDescent.lean). The block
254	  % `def:gr_modules_glueRestrictionIso` in Picard_GrassmannianQuot.tex is a stale
255	  % forward-declaration pinning the SAME Lean target and should be reconciled by the
256	  % plan agent (flagged in the writer report) so the pin is not duplicated.
257	  \uses{thm:isIso_glueRestrictionHom}
258	  Let \(\mathcal{M}\) be the glued sheaf of \cref{def:scheme_modules_glue}, with the
259	  transition family \((g_{ij})\) satisfying (C1)/(C2). For each chart index \(i\) the
260	  restriction morphism \(\mathrm{r}_i\) (\cref{lem:glueRestrictionHom}) is promoted to
261	  the \emph{restriction isomorphism}
262	  \[
263	    \rho_i \;:\; \iota_i^{*}\,\mathcal{M} \;\xrightarrow{\ \sim\ }\; \mathcal{M}_i,
264	  \]
265	  the canonical identification of the chart restriction of the glued sheaf with the
266	  \(i\)-th input sheaf, packaged from \cref{thm:isIso_glueRestrictionHom}. Its
267	  underlying morphism is the adjoint transpose of the \(i\)-th descent-equalizer
268	  projection, and over each overlap \(V_{ij}\) the two restrictions \(\rho_i, \rho_j\)
269	  differ exactly by the transition isomorphism \(g_{ij}\).
270	\end{definition}

# Analogy: realising G1-core `isLocalizedModule_basicOpen_of_isQuasicoherent` (QC affine descent)

## Mode
api-alignment

## Slug
g1core-descent

## Iteration
028

## Question
Build, axiom-clean, `Scheme.Modules.isLocalizedModule_basicOpen_of_isQuasicoherent`: for a
quasi-coherent `M : (Spec R).Modules` and `f : R`, the restriction `Γ(M,⊤) → Γ(M,D(f))` is
`IsLocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers f)` (Stacks 01HA / Hartshorne II.5.2). Is the planned
explicit finite-cover + flat-equalizer descent the cheapest realisation, or does Mathlib's
`Scheme.Modules`/`QuasicoherentData`/`Tilde`/`SheafOfModules` API make a shorter affine descent
cheap? Confirm the lemma names for the chosen route.

## Project artifact(s)
- `AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/QuotScheme.lean:467` — `isLocalizedModule_tilde_restrict` (built):
  restriction of `tilde N` localizes.
- `AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/QuotScheme.lean:510` — `isLocalizedModule_restrict_of_isIso_fromTildeΓ`
  (built): section-localization for `M` *given* `[IsIso M.fromTildeΓ]`.
- `AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/QuotScheme.lean:614,653` — `isIso_fromTildeΓ_of_isLocalizedModule_restrict`
  / `isIso_fromTildeΓ_iff_isLocalizedModule_restrict` (built): the **G1-assemble** glue. Everything
  downstream is now gated on this one missing keystone (G1-core), which is the directive's target.

## The decisive precedent (the heart of this consult)

Mathlib already proves the **structure-sheaf** version of this exact statement, and proves it
**not** by a finite-equalizer + flat-descent of modules:

> `AlgebraicGeometry.isLocalization_basicOpen_of_qcqs`
> (`Mathlib/AlgebraicGeometry/Morphisms/QuasiSeparated.lean:389`) [verified] — the "**Qcqs lemma**"
> of Vakil: for a qcqs scheme `X` and `f : Γ(X,U)`, `Γ(X, D(f)) ≃ Γ(X,U)_f`, i.e.
> `IsLocalization.Away f Γ(X, D(f))`.

Its proof (read in full) is the template we should copy. It assembles the *ring*
`IsLocalization.Away` from its **three pointwise axioms**, each discharged by a pointwise
existence lemma proved by **compact-open induction over the affine cover**, gluing two opens at a
time with the **sheaf condition** — never building an equalizer object, never invoking flatness:

- map_units: `IsUnit.pow _ (RingedSpace.isUnit_res_basicOpen _ f)`
  (`Mathlib/Geometry/RingedSpace/Basic.lean:161`) [verified].
- surj: `exists_eq_pow_mul_of_isCompact_of_isQuasiSeparated` (QuasiSeparated.lean:306) [verified] —
  "every section on `D(f)` is `f^{-n}·`(a section on `U`)". Proved by
  `compact_open_induction_on` (`Mathlib/AlgebraicGeometry/Morphisms/QuasiCompact.lean:235`)
  [verified]: base = affine open via `IsAffineOpen.isLocalization_basicOpen`; step = glue `S` and
  affine `U` via the sheaf gluing gadget `objSupIsoProdEqLocus` + `eq_of_locally_eq'`.
- exists_of_eq: `exists_pow_mul_eq_zero_of_res_basicOpen_eq_zero_of_isCompact` (QuasiSeparated.lean)
  [verified] — the kernel-up-to-power statement, same induction.

The target `IsLocalizedModule` unfolds to the **identical three pointwise axioms**:
`IsLocalizedModule` (`Mathlib/Algebra/Module/LocalizedModule/Basic.lean:525`) [verified] is the
class `⟨map_units, surj, exists_of_eq⟩`. So the *module* keystone is the module analogue of the
qcqs lemma, with the same proof skeleton.

196	def appLE (U : Y.Opens) (V : X.Opens) (e : V ≤ f ⁻¹ᵁ U) : Γ(Y, U) ⟶ Γ(X, V) :=

203	  rfl

208	  appLE_map _ _ _
209

213	      f.appLE U' V (e.trans ((Opens.map f.base).map i.unop).le) := by

215	  rfl

11	
12	/-!
13	# The category of presheaves of modules over a scheme
14	
15	In this file, given a scheme `X`, we define the category of presheaves
16	of modules over `X`. As categories of presheaves of modules are
17	defined for presheaves of rings (and not presheaves of commutative rings),
18	we also introduce a definition `X.ringCatSheaf` for the underlying sheaf
19	of rings of `X`.
20	
21	-/
22	
23	@[expose] public section
24	
25	universe u
26	
27	open CategoryTheory
28	
29	namespace AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme
30	
31	variable (X Y : Scheme.{u})
32	
33	/-- The underlying sheaf of rings of a scheme. -/
34	abbrev ringCatSheaf : TopCat.Sheaf RingCat.{u} X :=
35	  (sheafCompose _ (forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat.{u})).obj X.sheaf
36	
37	/-- The category of presheaves of modules over a scheme. -/
38	nonrec abbrev PresheafOfModules := PresheafOfModules.{u} X.ringCatSheaf.obj
39	
40	variable {X Y} in
41	/-- The morphism of sheaves of rings corresponding to a morphism of schemes. -/
42	def Hom.toRingCatSheafHom (f : X ⟶ Y) :
43	    Y.ringCatSheaf ⟶ ((TopologicalSpace.Opens.map f.base).sheafPushforwardContinuous
44	      _ _ _).obj X.ringCatSheaf where
45	  hom := Functor.whiskerRight f.c _
46	
47	end AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme

input.new_string:
/-- `appLE` transport along an equality of morphisms: for equal `f = g` the induced
section maps `Γ(B, U) ⟶ Γ(A, W)` agree (the open-inequality witnesses are
proof-irrelevant). Generic `subst` helper for the overlap structure-sheaf
compatibility. Project-local. -/
lemma appLE_congr_mor {A B : Scheme.{u}} {f g : A ⟶ B} (h : f = g) (U : B.Opens)
    (W : A.Opens) (e : W ≤ f ⁻¹ᵁ U) (e' : W ≤ g ⁻¹ᵁ U) :
    f.appLE U W e = g.appLE U W e' := by
  subst h; rfl

/-- **Structure-sheaf compatibility of the chart-overlap square**: the two composite
section maps `Γ(U_i, V) ⟶ Γ(U_j, (t_ij ≫ f_ji) '' (f_ij⁻¹ V))` of the overlap square —
"through the glued scheme" (via `(ι_i.appIso V)⁻¹`, `ι_j.app`, and the opens identity
`glueData_preimage_image_eq`) and "through the overlap" (via `f_ij.app` and
`((t_ij ≫ f_ji).appIso)⁻¹`) — coincide. Both sides are the `appLE` of the two (equal)
composites `(t_ij ≫ f_ji) ≫ ι_j = f_ij ≫ ι_i` of the square; this is the
`pushforwardCongr` coherence consumed by `glueOverlapBaseChangeIso`. Project-local. -/
lemma glueData_overlap_appIso_compat (D : Scheme.GlueData.{0}) (i j : D.J)
    (V : (D.U i).Opens) :
    ((D.ι i).appIso V).inv ≫ (D.ι j).app ((D.ι i) ''ᵁ V) ≫
        (D.U j).presheaf.map (eqToHom (glueData_preimage_image_eq D i j V).symm).op
      = (D.f i j).app V ≫ ((D.t i j ≫ D.f j i).appIso ((D.f i j) ⁻¹ᵁ V)).inv := by
  have hsq : (D.t i j ≫ D.f j i) ≫ D.ι j = D.f i j ≫ D.ι i := by
    rw [Category.assoc]; exact D.glue_condition i j
  rw [Iso.eq_comp_inv]
  simp only [Category.assoc]
  rw [Iso.inv_comp_eq]
  simp only [Scheme.Hom.appIso_hom', Scheme.Hom.app_eq_appLE, Scheme.Hom.appLE_map,
    Scheme.Hom.appLE_map_assoc, Scheme.Hom.appLE_comp_appLE]
  exact appLE_congr_mor hsq _ _ _ _

/-- **Overlap base change of the chart square** (`β_ij`): restricting a pushforward
